# Feature: Run Reporting — Collection Run HTML Report

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-003
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_run_reporting.md`

First end-to-end vertical slice of the run reporting feature. Adds an "Export Report" button to [RunCollectionModal.tsx](packages/core/src/components/common/RunCollectionModal.tsx) that, on click, builds a single-file HTML report of the current collection run and downloads it via the `exportFile` adapter (FEAT-001).

After this feature ships:

- A user can run a collection from the Run Collection modal and download a self-contained, interactive HTML report of that run.
- The HTML report is openable in any browser offline, with click-to-expand request cards and Request / Response / Validation tabs.
- Subsequent flow and test-suite reports (FEAT-004, FEAT-005) reuse all the infrastructure landed here.

---

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

`.spec-lite/data_model.md` does not exist. This feature adds two new types and one hook with local UI state — none persisted.

### Entities & Attributes

- **`CollectionReportInput`** (new type in `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/builders/collectionRun.ts`)
  - `metadata` (`ReportMetadata`) — populated with `runType: 'collection'`, the modal's collection name + `itemPath`, the chosen environment / default auth names, the `RunSettings.concurrentCalls` and `RunSettings.delayBetweenCalls`, and the run start/completed timestamps captured from the runner.
  - `summary` (`ReportSummary`) — derived from the runner's `progress` and `averageTime`.
  - `items` (`ReportRequestNode[]`) — built from the modal's `displayRequests` after merging in the runner's results (the same merge that already happens in the modal via `applyRunnerResult`).

- **`ReportExportStatus`** (new type in `packages/core/src/hooks/useReportExport.ts`)
  - `state` (`'idle' | 'generating' | 'success' | 'error'`).
  - `error` (`string | undefined`) — surfaced in `state: 'error'`.
  - `lastFileName` (`string | undefined`) — populated on success for any UI that wants to surface "saved as <name>".

- **`useReportExport` hook return** (new)
  - `status` (`ReportExportStatus`).
  - `exportCollectionRun(input: CollectionReportInput): Promise<void>` — the public method introduced by this feature.

### Relationships

- N/A.

### Indexes & Constraints

- The Export button is disabled when (a) the runner is currently `isRunning`, or (b) no requests have results yet (`progress.completed === 0`), or (c) `status.state === 'generating'`.

---

## 3. Files

- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/builders/collectionRun.ts` — `buildCollectionRunReport(input: CollectionReportInput): string`.
- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/index.ts` — Re-export the builder and the input type.
- `packages/core/src/hooks/useReportExport.ts` — New hook, owns local export status, exposes `exportCollectionRun`.
- `packages/core/src/components/common/RunCollectionModal.tsx` — Add Export button + status indicator; wire to the new hook.
- `packages/core/src/test/utils/reporting/builders/collectionRun.test.ts`

- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/RunCollectionModal.test.tsx` — Extend (or create) covering the new button.

---

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-001** — `IStorageAdapter.exportFile` is the I/O entry point for this hook.
- **FEAT-002** — `renderShell`, `renderHeader`, `renderSummary`, `renderRequestDetail`, `buildReportFilename`, types from the foundation module.

---

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Implement `buildCollectionRunReport`

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/builders/collectionRun.ts`, implement `buildCollectionRunReport(input)` that composes `renderShell({ title: \`Wave Collection Run — ${metadata.subjectName}\`, body })` where `body` is the concatenation of `renderHeader(metadata)`, `renderSummary(summary)`, and `<section class="items">` containing `items.map(renderRequestDetail).join('')`. Order matches the runner's `displayRequests` order. The collection report is **flat** — no folder grouping (folder paths are surfaced in the request-detail card via the existing `folderPath` field, identical to `RunRequestCard`).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `collectionRun.test.ts` covers (1) happy path: 3 mixed-status items → output contains 3 request-detail cards, summary tiles match, header shows collection name + concurrency/delay. (2) Empty `items` → output still produces a valid shell with summary showing zeros. (3) XSS regression: feed `<script>alert(1)</script>` into the metadata `subjectName` and an item `name` — assert no executable `<script>` substring. (4) Output is a valid stand-alone HTML document (starts with `<!DOCTYPE html>`, contains exactly one `<head>` and one `<body>`).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the builder + the input type. Update `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/index.ts` to re-export.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- reporting/builders/collectionRun` passes. Manually open the produced HTML in a browser; expand a card, switch tabs, confirm interactivity works.

### TASK-002: Implement `useReportExport` hook with `exportCollectionRun`

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `packages/core/src/hooks/useReportExport.ts`, implement the hook. It uses `useStorageAdapter()` and `useNotificationAdapter()`. Local state is a single `useState<ReportExportStatus>({ state: 'idle' })`. The `exportCollectionRun(input)` method: (1) sets state to `generating`, (2) calls `buildCollectionRunReport(input)`, (3) calls `adapter.storage.exportFile(buildReportFilename('collection', metadata.subjectName, new Date()), html, 'text/html')`, (4) on `Result.isOk` sets state to `success` with `lastFileName`, (5) on `Result.isErr` sets state to `error` with the message, calls `notification.showNotification('error', \`Failed to export report: ${error}\`)`, (6) wraps the entire body in `try/catch` so an unexpected throw from the builder also lands in the `error` branch (the foundation module promises not to throw, but defense-in-depth — and the user explicitly asked for "proper status indicator and error in case html generator runs into unexpected error"). Returns `Promise<void>` (no result needed; UI reads `status`).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `useReportExport.test.tsx` using `renderHook` and a mocked adapter (via `createMockAdapter()`): (1) initial state is `idle`. (2) Successful adapter call: state transitions `idle → generating → success`, `lastFileName` matches the filename passed to `exportFile`. (3) Adapter returns `err`: state transitions `idle → generating → error`, `notification.showNotification('error', ...)` is called once with a message containing the error string. (4) Builder throws unexpectedly (force by injecting an item that breaks the builder via mock spy): state ends in `error`, no unhandled rejection. (5) `status.state === 'idle'` after the next successful call (state transitions reset cleanly between calls).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the hook + each method. Update `packages/core/src/index.ts` (or whatever public surface file `packages/core` exposes) to re-export the hook for adapter packages that may want to compose it.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- useReportExport` passes.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001.

### TASK-003: Wire Export button into `RunCollectionModal`

- [ ] **Implementation**: In [RunCollectionModal.tsx](packages/core/src/components/common/RunCollectionModal.tsx), add an Export button (`SecondaryButton` with Lucide `DownloadIcon`) to the top control row, placed after the Run/Stop button. Disabled when (a) `runner.isRunning`, (b) `runner.progress.completed === 0`, or (c) `reportStatus.state === 'generating'`. While generating, swap the icon for a spinning `Loader2` and disable the button. On click, build a `CollectionReportInput`: pull `metadata` from existing modal state (`collectionName + itemPath` for `subjectName`, `selectedEnvironmentId` resolved against `environments` for `environmentName`, `selectedAuthId` resolved against `auths` for `defaultAuthName`, `settings.concurrentCalls` / `settings.delayBetweenCalls`, started/completed/totalElapsed from the runner's progress timestamps), build `summary` from `metrics` (already computed in the modal), build `items` from the existing `displayRequests` array (already merged with runner results) — only including selected requests (filter by `selectedRequestIds`). Pass to `useReportExport().exportCollectionRun(input)`. After success, also display a brief inline check-mark icon next to the button for ~3 seconds (use a `useEffect` that resets `status` to `idle` after a timeout — implement on the component side rather than baking timeouts into the hook).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `RunCollectionModal.test.tsx` extensions: (1) Export button is rendered. (2) Button is disabled when `runner.isRunning`, when `progress.completed === 0`, and when status is `generating`. (3) Click invokes `exportCollectionRun` once with correctly-shaped input — assert metadata fields populated from selected env / auth / settings, items array contains only selected requests, item count equals `selectedRequestIds.size`. (4) On success, the button shows a confirmation indicator. (5) On error, an error notification is surfaced (assertion via mocked notification adapter).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the new button-related props or local state. Update the [RunCollectionModal.tsx](packages/core/src/components/common/RunCollectionModal.tsx) component-level comment to mention the export capability.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- RunCollectionModal` passes. Manual smoke: launch the dev VS Code host, run a small collection, click Export, confirm the file downloads and opens in a browser with working interactivity.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002.

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: The report header surfaces the *name* of the default auth profile (option (a)). Credential values are never accessed during export.
- **Error Handling**: All adapter calls go through `Result<T, E>` and the hook surfaces failures via the existing notification adapter. The Export button never throws to React's error boundary — defense-in-depth `try/catch` in the hook catches unexpected builder errors.
- **Logging**: The builder is pure (no logs). The hook does not log success / failure (the notification adapter is the user-facing surface). Adapter-side logging is FEAT-001's responsibility.

---
